boardgame: Monty Python's Really Silly Board Game
description:
If you’re a fan of Monty Python or just plain silly, then this Really Silly Board Game will have you laughing like Ernest Scribbler as you travel the board collecting Spam. Test your knowledge of Monty Python TV and movie trivia, undertake silly challenges and explore the wickedly funny world of Monty Python.

- A board game for adults combining Monty Python trivia, charades, impressions and silly behaviour
- Features 6 characters: Gumby, Knight of Ni, Spanish Inquisitor, Mr Creosote, Lumberjack & Brian of Nazareth
- Trade Spam pieces for Quest items and complete your quest to win the game
- 400 silly questions and hilarious tasks such as ‘Who’s got the funniest silly walk?'
- An ideal break from the usual Christmas ‘bored’ game!

In the game, up to six bears try to create a store of honey. On the field, around a meadow, are six bear caves. In the middle of the meadow there are the bees. Every player has a choice to move the bears or the bees.The bears move exactly four steps, and if they cross one, they can lift honey pots, which they try as quickly as possible to transport to their caves. When moving the bee you roll a special die (2x honey symbol 3,4,5,6). The honey bee is then moved to a space and on their old space is a honey pot is laid. The bees can also chase the bears, if caught the affected bears lose the honey and start again in the cave. Honey which is already in the cave is safe. The winner is the bear who has a full stock (adjusted according to players) collected first.

"Hello Mr. Bear" is part of the Haba series "From 2 years," an infants-game series from Haba. The game is several games within a game. The game has no board but rather it is made up of a house that can be built up, it has three walls and an open side, and several wooden figures that can be positioned within the house. The first game supports listening and understanding of words and figures and the fine motor skills. Other games are simple memory games where the children try to remember which figures have been placed in the house.

Luca and his white dog Baldur are to look after the tinted colour-sheep. The little shepherd leads the green sheep to a very green meadow, but what does that yellow sheep need? A book for reading out loud, to look at, to tell stories, a story on colours and tinted sheep, and a game in which to roll the die and thread the sheep onto a woollen string. Luca moves from spot to spot, if all the sheep and the dog are threaded before the sun sinks, all animals can sleep in the barn and the game is won.

Idéspelet is both a board game and a creativity tool. In the game the players create ideas around a given question and pitch-battle these against each other. The greatest ideas advance in rank and the weaker go back for another iteration and another chance. In the end the player with the highest ranking is proclaimed the winner.

From the rules:
Taxibo is a unique Surinamese board game, which takes you by taxi through the centre of Paramaribo. It is a game for 2 to 4 players and consists of 1 dice, 16 pieces in 4 colours and a board.

From the website:
First Surinamese boardgame, developed by Gerrit Baron for the whole family. Taxibo is a taxi game which takes place in Paramaribo. Each of the four players owns a taxi company, who's four cars stand in four different garages. very exciting and above all educational.

Discription: Taxibo is a basic role and move game with some similarities to Sorry. Players move their pawns (taxi's) by rolling dice. The board represents (in an very abstract manner) the streets of Paramaribo. The end goal is getting all four taxi's to the central square. The players who first achieves this wins. Some of the spaces of the board are related to famous sights in Paramaribo and require a player who lands on them to do things such as skip turns, roll again, etc.

From publisher:

The Island of Fear 
Can you survive until sunrise?

A plane carrying a group of tourists to the Maldives crashes in the Indian Ocean. On the first night after the crash, the survivors realize that there were werewolves on the plane with them. These fiends organized the whole crash so that they could hunt down some humans in the moonlight. There is only one way to survive until the rescue crew arrives – find out who the werewolves are and lynch them.

The Island of Fear is probably the most advanced version of the world-renowned Mafia game. First of all, in addition to the normal tourists and werewolves, there is a sexual predator, a clairvoyant, a nurse, a witch, an untouchable, a nightwalker, a half-blood werewolf and even a were-hamster. All sorts of nasty surprises can be expected from this colorful group of people.  Second, the game contains a set of event cards that create specific events as the game progresses. Third, there are object cards that can both help or hinder the owner. Finally, the game comes with a DVD with mood-setting backgrounds and a timer for day-time voting. Also included are a chart and voting tokens to help manage the game.

description:
Nobody knows who or when, but one player is going to be the killer! Discretion and assassination attempt on one side, and vigileance stalking each other. Suspense guaranteed!

Assassin X is based on a user specific trading card (55 originals cards) through which you build your game and influence your opponents. It is a game of strategy and psychology in which deception and bluff you will need to win!

The goal of the game is the first player to have 5 pts.  You gains points if 
1) Successful murder = 2 points
2) Countering murder = 1 point
3) Discover an assassin = 1 point

Each player receives 5 cards that they keep secret.
The rest is the deck face down. A sense of play is defined for a turn. It will be inverted at each new turn.
Each round takes place in two stages:
- A phase of trading between 2 players.
- A phase of action for any player

If you receive the card or draw Assassin
you become a murderer. Unless you try to get rid of it, you should prepare your murder by collecting maximum points with maps of Crime Guns, Approaches,and Masters. However, be careful not to be detect because it may well be another player sends you the Police and there, it would be over.
Assassinate when you're ready!

boardgame: Silent War: IJN – Imperial Japanese Navy
description:
(from Compass Games website:)

Bring added realism to your submarine campaign with IJN, Imperial Japanese Navy, the first expansion pack for Compass Games’ award winning SILENT WAR.

Every Japanese warship down through the large destroyer escorts and the major auxiliary cargo ships and tankers are provided in two full countersheets with 336 new playing pieces for the game and rules to integrate them seamlessly into the game. IJN, Imperial Japanese Navy also brings new rules to SILENT WAR such as loners, crash dive, and targeting escorts, rules introduced in the follow on game, STEEL WOLVES.

A must have expansion pack for any dedicated SILENT WAR player. Requires SILENT WAR to play.

description:
Description from publisher:

Un juego "pipí cucú" para revivir lo más divertido de la época.

En este juego, los setentosos y ochentosos, tienen que contestar  preguntas, actuar, dibujar, silbar y mucho más...recordando lo más 
divertido de la época.

¿Te animás a actuar representando al "Super agente 86" y dibujar un balero...o al Correcaminos; y a silbar "Menta y Limón"... o "Cleopatra"?


Is a mix between Trivial Pursuit, Pictionary and acting, everything based on the music, tv, movies and culture of the 70s and 80s.  Players must act, answer questions, draw, whistle, and sing in various competitions.

User summary:

A religious educational board game that introduces Catholics to the saints and their missions. Intended to help players understand God's calling in their own lives. Includes a book on the 76 featured saints.

Players move their pewter figurines around the board a la Monopoly. When landing on a color-coded square they answer one of four questions read from corresponding cards:

Blue - Light or humorous
Green - Scripture memory
Red - Thoughtful, reflective
Yellow - Lives of the saints

The first person to fully navigate the board by successfully answering questions wins.

The game takes about an hour to play and is geared to ages 10 and up. The goal is to learn more about one's faith, one's mission in life, and about the calling in other players' lives.

User Summary

Age of Frederick the Great - Wargame Rules for the Seven Years War is a miniatures rules set that includes a card-driven resolution system.

Rules cover sequence of play, movement, terrain effects, fire, melee, morale and rally, the leader action cards (and how to create them), game scale, and unit and army organization.

Rules set appears in Wargames Illustrated #178 (July 2002)

boardgame: What's Up, Doc? The Bugs Bunny Game
description:
From the box front: Raid Elmer Fudd's vegetable patch and collect the most carrots

User summary: At the start of the game, players get a supply of carrots.  On each turn, every player gets a card.  When any two vegetable cards match, everyone grabs for a Bugs Bunny piece, with enough for every player but one (i.e. three Bugs pieces for four players).  Whoever does not get a Bugs piece must give a carrot to the other players.  In addition, there are three Elmer Fudd cards.  If one of these turns up, no one should grab for Bugs.  If they do touch Bugs, they must give up two carrots to each other player.  The same penalty applies for touching Bugs when there are no matching cards.

When one player runs out of carrots, the game is over, and the player with the most carrots wins.

From the Introduction

"Last year I promised to present for discussion a set of rules covering the Armada period of naval warfare. Here are those rules, or rather a fast play version of the Channel Wargames Group's current set.

"The philosophy behind them is both to make an attempt to make a set of rules that give a good flavour of the period and the methods of fighting with ships current at that time. And to make the games played with them as enjoyable as is possible. Because of this things such as gunnery have been simplified as much as possible.

"Ships gunnery at the time of the Armada was a thing in a state of flux. It is as well to contemplate, that ships carried many different types of ordinance, all intermingled together on the same deck. The logistics of serving such a varied armament in a rapid manner was beyond the capabilities of the time, and I would hazard a guess beyond us today as well. Most authorities give a maximum rate of fire for the guns of the Spaniards as once, yes once, a day. Small pieces and hand held weapons were obviously much quicker to reload. Even the English rate of fire is said to have been at best once an hour. If this difference had been used in the rules the Spanish player's would be sitting on their for much of the game, and the English for quite long periods. The solution to this problem can be read in the rules themselves..."

User Summary

Rules set covers scale, speed of units, sailing rules, handling classes, wind effects, firing, ranges, firing charts, special effects table (effectively a sort of critical hit table), putting out fires, effects of bowing on hull factors, boarding, morale, special rules for galleys, a ship list, and a brief bibliography. Game requires models (1/600 preferred for ease of scratch building), and two types of dice (d6 & d20).

Rules set appears in Wargames Illustrated #178 (July 2002)

A gorgeous game in resin, coming in a huge wooden box. Two players or teams must dig for the Pharao's treasure, finding hints that will help them in the search. The valley of the Sphinx is made of little 3D spaces in resin that can be lifted to see what's under. A guide with the reproduction of real ancient Egyptian documents is featired to translate the hieroglyphics and identify Pharaos' names. The cost was impressive (150.000 liras - equivalent of 77.50 euros that was a really huge sum in mid '90s).

boardgame: Hour of the Gun: Tombstone, Arizona, 1881-2
description:
User Summary

Hour of the Gun - Tombstone, Arizona, 1881-2 is a set of three linked miniatures scenarios and a rules set that covers the events surrounding the infamous gunfight at the OK Corral. Scenarios include: Street Fight at the Ok Corral, Tucson Stockyards Ambush, & Shootout at Iron Springs. Characters surviving a scenario (from either the Earp or Clanton faction) are eligible to appear in the next scenario. Off table events (like an assassination attempt on the Earps) between the scenarios help to build suspense.

Effectively this is a set of skirmish scenarios with characters that are drawn from history. Characters that didn't appear at the events in question, but that might have, are included in charts of "Improbables." Historical figures that did participate are found in the "Probable" charts, and have a much higher chance of participating. Percentile dice are used to determine the characters in the various scenarios.

Each character has their own set of characteristics which include: Reaction, Control, Bravery, Dexterity, Agility, Physical, Panic Distance, Security Distance, Pistol Skill, Rifle Skill, Hand Weapon Skill, & Fists Skill.

Game requires various old west miniatures and terrain, along with 2d10 for percentile rolls and single d10 rolls.

Rules set appears in Wargames Illustrated #178 (July 2002)

Russian card game of attacking and getting rid of all cards. Originally a game for two, three or four players, it's mostly known today in an adapted version for two players brought into England by the Mathematics professor A.S. Besikovitch, and later included in David Parlett's Oxford Guide to Card Games. This version is played with exposed cards and symmetrical hands, giving it a strong strategic flavour.

At the beginning of the (Besikovitch) game each player chooses two sets without overlapping with the other player, typically in a 32-card deck, out of which one is their own trump. Cards are dealt for the first player, who keeps the cards of their both sets; the second player gets exactly the same cards in the other sets and both their hands are filled up with the rest of the cards. As an example: a player who's chosen spades and clubs with spades as trumps keeps the cards in both sets and gives all their red cards together with the undealt cards to the other player, who then selects the analog cards to the first player's in the red sets with hearts as trumps, gives the rest of the red cards back to the first player and finally keeps all the remaining black cards. Both hands are symmetrical, and play can start.

From now on each turn a player lays a card in a pile between both players; before the next player can play out a card of their choice they must throw one that is superior in value to the top card in the pile or a trump. If the player can't or doesn't want to do so they must take the whole pile and forfeit the turn. So, each player throws two cards in a row: one following the previous card, and one to be followed by the opponent. The first player to get rid of all cards in their hand is the winner.

From the box: "A Game With No Redeeming Qualities"

A roll and move drinking game where you move your piece around a game board arranged like a person's home. The object of the game is to be the first player to get from the front door to the bathroom.

This is a TV tie-in with the popular eponymous German TV series.

Players move around the board trying to collect 1 chip from each of the 6 houses on the street. To collect a chip, a player has to answer a question concerning the show correctly. After collecting all 6 chips, the player needs to answer a master question correctly to win.

There are several inbuilt variants for more or less competitive/cut-throat play.

boardgame: Star Fleet Battles: Module Y3 – Early Years III
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Module Y3 comprises a 100-page SSD book, including: 
Early battleships for the Federation, Kzintis, and Gorns.

Warp-refitted command cruisers for the rest of the Federation member states before the unified star fleet was formed.

Warp-driven dreadnoughts for the five member species of the Inter-Stellar Concordium before they coalesced into one nation.

The 64-page rulebook includes five scenarios as well as the ship descriptions and annexes.

boardgame: Lost Complete Victory: Legend of the Galactic Heroes
description:
This is a Japanese science fiction wargame set in the universe of the Legend of the Galactic Heroes.

Two scenarios are contained in the game, and the fictional space battle that each was drawn in the story is reproduced. These are being called a fleet battle, but when either is said, it is close to the land battle of Napoleonic age.

Each player control one or more star fleets. 
Each fleet is commanded by one admiral, and organized by 10 to 20 ships units.

Each turn, a fleet are activated in the unintentional order by chit-pull system, and it resolves a movement and a combat. 
An average admiral has only one or two chits. But the heroes of the story has four or more chits, and it is activated  many times during one turn. 
A battle is resolved with adjacent hex on the Odds CRT.

The outcome is decided by the number which made a fleet collapse. A fleet collapses when a fixed unit is eliminate or admiral's mental agitation.

description:
This is self-published expansion kit for 独眼竜政宗 (Dokuganryu Masamune). Rulebook and reference card from 秀吉軍記 (Hideyoshi Gunki) or later game are needed.
"Mutsu Nikki" means "Record of the Mutsu province".

Covering battles in the Mutsu and Dewa provinces.
Six scenarios are included in this kit. "Battle of Eiroku 1", "Battle of Eiroku 2", "Punitive expedition to Ooura" and "Battle of Minato" are short games for two players. "Three champions of Dewa" is a short three players game. "Mutsu Nikki" is a campaign game for three or four players.

This is expansion kit for 信玄上洛 (Shingen Joraku). also needs 関東制圧 (Kanto Seiatsu), 信長風雲録 (Nobunaga Fuun-roku), 秀吉軍記 (Hideyoshi Gunki), The Longest Day in Sengoku.

Covering battles of Oda-Tokugawa alliance versus Takeda clan.

Two scenarios are included in this kit:

    "Shingen's Last Battle 2nd Edition" is expansion version of "信玄上洛 (Shingen Joraku). Large campaign scenario for two players. One player controls Takeda, Hojo clans and western coalition. The other player controls Oda, Tokugawa and Uesugi clans.
    "Battle of Nagashino - Nobunaga's Victory" is short game for two players.

boardgame: Cars: Radiator Springs Rallye Small Expansion
description:
Small expansion for the base game:

It comes with 4 new cars:
Ramone
Luigi
Guido
The King

Allows now the game to played up to 10 players.

And adds a new route as well called: "Even Longer". (Just adds 2 more tiles, with Luigi and The King special places.)

Expansion to Activity with 220 new cards. Themes: leisure and food

The gameboard and pawns are needed from one of the basic Activity games.
Most of the new cards have book-, film- or play titels (theatre, opera, music), fairy tales or poems to be guessed by the teams 
Also tasks about art and architecture, everything around food and kitchen and last but not least - games are includet in this theme pack.
It's a real challenge, to "dance" or picture the title of a poem, you might have learned in school - but will your teammates remember it too?

The minimum age is specified with 12, but 30 to 40 is best "experience-level"

A computation game for 2-4 players from 8-14 years

Contents: 4 men, 4 boards - each showing a pair of scales with adjustable weight figures, 60 tiles showing weight units from 1-60, track with 22 spaces.

1. Zero! Each player adjusts his scale so that it shows zero.
2. Two! Each player draws two weight-tiles and puts one on each side of his scale. It is up to hom which tile goes on the left and which tile goes on the right.
3. Quanto! Players draw a third tile an can place it either on the right or left side of their scales.
4. Got it! The first player who succeeds in balancing his scale by adjusting the dials scores most points

Football simulation for 2 to 6 players. The game consists of defense cards, midfield cards, attack cards and action cards. Each move is started by playing a midfield card. The cards have a color code that determines which kinds of cards can be played next (i.e. an attack card (red) can only be played if the previous card contains a red color code). Attack cards also show a number of balls that has to be surpassed by the next player. A player who cannot counter an attack card catches a goal and has to take all the cards from the current stack; the last player to play an attack card before that takes a scoring card. When the card stack is used up, the player with most scoring cards is the winner. 
Action cards can be used to reverse player order, pass an attack to the next player, etc.

(User summary by HilkMAN)

boardgame: Die kleine Hexe: nach dem Buch von Ottfried Preußler
description: In this game - following the children's book of Ottfried Preußler with the same name, each player will play the little sorceress once. The little sorceress tries to break the evil witches operations. So she attempts to burn the grimoires and witches' brooms. That child, whichs acts best, will win.

The XVII. the king scented the plans of the noblemen conspiring against it in England on a century's waist, that down it wants to be repelled from the throne. He deprived all those, who were getting involved in a suspicion, of their title, their property and their name because of this. As a last opportunity, offered it to the ones driven into the exile in order for them to prove their innocence, that who gets into the utmost nook of his realm as the first, it proves it with his desperation, worthy that way, that gets back his goods taken away. Your rivals will make your road harder, save up at what more thalers, that give yourself to benefits and be able to hold back your adversaries.

Be the most resolute one and reach the aim, the long one and tiring one firstly on a road, defying the obstacles, that regain all that, from which falsely bereft!

Peregrinus is a game that is still under development and just before publishing. Consequently, the rules are not complete yet but are still changing. In the game you have to come up with different colour combinations with four cards, and you can proceed in the game with these combinations. You can collect money, points, action cards and treasures. All this will be converted into victory points at the end of the game. You can create obstacles for your opponents, and you can turn the action cards into your advantage but can also annoy your opponents with these cards.

The aim of the game is to collect the most VPs

boardgame: Life of Christ: Bible Journeys No. 2
description:
Follow the life of Christ from Bethlehem to Calvary.  Players move along a path that follows the events of the life of Christ and answer questions on cards that are drawn.  First player to the end wins.

User Summary

This is a game for 2 players. You need 1 set of Nestortiles.

Each player chooses 5 colours and takes the corresponding tiles.
One player begins by placing a tile valued 1 on to the table. Then the players continue alternately.

On his turn a player puts 1 to 3 (dependent on the move type) tiles onto the table. There are 6 possibilities to extend the building:

(Floor tiles are tiles directly on the table, wall tiles are vertical tiles and top tiles are horizontal tiles not touching the table)


    Floor - A tile directly on the table touching at least one other tile.
    Corner Wall - Two adjacent vertical tiles with an inbetween angle of 90°, both touching a floor tile.
    Single Wall - A vertical tile touching at least one other wall tile. It also has to touch a floor or top tile.
    Tunnel - The top tile has to touch exactly one wall tile, and may additionally touch one or more top tiles. On the opposite side of the touching wall side another wall is placed.
    Single Top - A top tile touching at least 2 wall tiles.
    Corner Top - A top tile touching at least 2 other top tiles but no wall tile. On every side not touching another top tile a wall tile is added.


There are some additional restrictions for the moves:

The players have to use the tiles colour by colour. Only if the first used colour is completely placed on the table, the next colour may be used. So if there is only one tile remaining in a colour the player can only place one tile in his next turn.  The colour by colour rule is meant to be for the tiles of one player. Example: Player A starts with a yellow tile. Player B continues with two green tiles. Now player A has to continue with yellow until all yellow is used. Only then player A may choose his next colour. (Same for player B with green)

Each placed tile has to touch at least one tile with the same or a smaller value.  The dots of the top tiles always must be on the top side. So it is not possible to have a top directly above a floor.

A player wins if he places all 30 tiles on the table.
A player loses if he has no legal move (Beware of tiles with small value).

Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #7.

Small card-driven game covering a famous battle during the Sengoku era.
In 1575, Katsuyori Takeda ((武田 勝頼) invaded Mikawa controlled by Ieyasu Tokugawa (徳川 家康). Nobunaga Oda decided to reinforce Tokugawa. He fought Takeda's traditional horsemen with innovative tactics and arquebusiers.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Nagashino

Takeda must conquer the Nagashino castle before the game ends and Tokugawa must prevent him to succeed.

Components:

    1 map
    80 counters
    4 pages rulebook
    30 cards
    2 dice

description:
Guided Lands - Tabletop battles for all fantasy miniatures

Guided Lands is a new tabletop game for two players, ages 14 and up. The game has an universal approach to tabletop gaming, where almost all fantasy miniatures with a scale around 28mm can be fielded in battle. Players take on the role of two mighty warlords, who muster their troops to claim the throne and be crowned king of the Guided Lands. Doing so players will use a unique step-by-step approach to create different races and equip their soldiers with weapons, armor and special abilities, thus making nearly all of your fantasy miniatures you have lying around somewhere ready to be used as heroes or units in Guided Lands. The scale ranges from small skirmishes to epic battles including monsters, magic and powerful artillery. An easy and streamlined ruleset as well as a card-driven initiative system keeps gameplay fast, while also engaging for seasoned tabletop generals. So dust of your old miniatures and start playing!

Guided Lands is the first game of the new publisher Smiling Monster Games and will be released in a limited first edition of 500 copies at Essen Spiel 2010 in German language (Hall 6, booth 6-808), estimated price € 20 (softcover book).

More information at www.smiling-monster.de

From the instructions booklet cover:

Educational game. Geography, History, folklore, industry and commerce of Spain.

Components: (User Summary):

-72 round pieces
-Map in six sections of 34x16cm each
-Rules booklet

Goal:

Players must fill the spaces in the map with the pieces corresponding to each location.

Game mechanics:

Players must fill the spaces in the map with the counters pulled out randomly from any opaque container after identyfing the correct location for each.
There are three levels of difficulty (low, medium, high).

Juego instructivo. Geografia, Historia, costumbres, industria y comercio de España.

Componentes:

-72 fichas circulares de cartón 
-Mapa en seis secciones rectangulares de 34x16cm cada una.
-Instrucciones

Objetivo:

Los jugadores deben rellenar el mapa con las fichas correspondientes a cada localización.

Mecánica de juego:

Existen tres niveles de dificultad (elemental, medio, superior).

A follow-up to Le Loto Des Odeurs, Le Loto Des Saveurs attempts to teach children about flavours.

The game contains 30 jars with tiny candies, and 30 cards with images of the different flavours. Each turn, one jar is opened and each player gets a candy. They now try to identify the flavour as quickly as possible. A player that wants to make a guess grabs the corresponding card, after which his guess is verified by the game master.

The game is won by the player who has guessed the most flavours correctly at the end of the game.

Zomboree is a large group social card game (6 - 16 people) where players take on the roles of various survivors in a town under seige by zombies.  Each turn is affected by an event card drawn for that day.  Each turn is divided into two phases: Day and Night.  A 90 second timer is provided with the game to provide a  fair length for both phases.

During setup, survivor cards will be handed out.  One of the survivors will be Patient Zero and become a zombie at the end of the first day.  Each survivor starts with one item.  After everyone has an item, the group elects their leader.  Since no one knows anyone else's role, it is possible to elect Patient Zero as the group's leader.  Anytime the leader becomes a zombie, the survivors must elect a new leader before beginning the day phase.

During the day the elected leader of the survivors draws resource cards and the survivors discuss how to divide them up.  Some of the Survivors, however, are infected and will eventually join the Zombies attacking the Survivors.  At the end of each day, players check their character cards to see if they have become a zombie.

During the night, the players who have become zombies play attack cards of various types against the remaining survivors.  The survivors must work together to defend themselves using the resources they found during the day to resist and shunt off zombie attacks in order to survive.  Those who cannot stop or deflect a zombie attack, become zombies who will get a zombie attack card the next night.

The game's scenario runs for one week's time, from Sunday to Saturday.  Each turn an event card shapes both the day and night phases.  Each day the survivors use their drawn resources to make a plan for resisting the zombies.  Each night the zombies attack the survivors and try to wipe them out.  The game ends after the night phase on Saturday when those still alive win by surviving the zombie apocalypse, or else when all survivors are   dead (or undead, as it were).

description:
Finally a card game about shopping, with pedagogic opportunities for dialogue, as well as super fun action with the sneaky SuperMark action cards. In this fun SuperMark game you will have to go shopping for your family. Each participant draws a shopping list, but do not think you'll be a certain winner, because as soon as you are closing in on the goods on your shopping list, an opponent can play the action card "switch shopping list", and then you will have to find the goods on that list.

The SuperMark card games come in five different variations, each one with its own theme. The games are especially good for pre-school children, as they will learn how to, purely motorically, hold the cards, and they get practice in colours, combinations, numbers and relations.

Card games don't come better than this. You'll each draw a post card and with the help of a few questions, observations and memory, you will have to try to give the post card to the right person.

The SuperMark card games come in five different variations, each with their own theme. The games are especially good for pre-school children, as they will learn how to, purely motorically, hold the cards, and they get practice in colours, combinations, numbers and relations.

SuperMark is a sympathetic and easy beginner game. The game where good deeds are rewarded. The optimal beginner card game, where the child has at most three cards on their hand at all times. In this fun card game you'll have to avert an accident. Play a accident card on the table, for example a boy who's about to jump in a swimming pool without water, and then find the hero card in which SuperMark pours water into the pool. The game consists of 40 super nice cards - with fun hero deeds.

The SuperMark card games come in five different variations, each with their own theme. The games are especially good for pre-school children, as they will learn how to, purely motorically, hold the cards, and they get practice in colours, combinations, numbers and relations.

This card game is played mainly in Afganistan and Iran. It was mentioned in an article in GAMES magazine, sometime in the 2000s. It is similar to, but differs a fair bit (scoring and key cards), from the card game known as Casino and another called "Bazra".

The game uses a standard 52-card deck, without jokers. After an initial deal of five cards to each person (2-player only), two cards are turned up in the middle of the table. After playing one card at a time from the hand, another 5 cards are dealt to the players once their current hand is empty. Thus, each game segment lasts 5 rounds (10 cards per round + the initial 2 card outlay).

Points (always 5 per game segment) are given at the end of the deck for the following:
Most cards = 2
Most clubs = 1
2 of clubs = 1
10 of diamonds = 1

See the A new 2-player card game (for most of you) for a visual explanation of rules and scoring basics.

Party game based on a German TV show. The game consists of cards with sentence starts and four possible endings. Each player also has a set of four guessing cards (A-D, one for each answer) and some heart chips. The sentences deal mostly with men/women stereotypes.
In each round, a player reads the sentence and the four endings, then each player chooses an answer option. Then pairs are assigned and the two players try to guess what answer the other player chose. The first successful pair exchanges heart chips. If only one player guessed correctly, he/she gets a single point. After 12 rounds, heart chips are counted, with multiple chips in the same color counting less than a diversity of colors. Bonus points are added and the player with most points is the winner.

(User summary by HilkMAN)

boardgame: Muchachada Nui: el juego de mesa

This is a card game based on a Spanish TV show of "strange" humor and parodies for 3-4 players. You'll find many characters from this program and have some laughs. In the game of Muchachada Nui the players, in the paper of surrealistic television producers, pay the cache of important Celebrities to gain hearing points. The first program that arrives to the maximum level of hearing will be proclaimed as the winner. Just play the cards to get points as you use your Celebrities and cards to stop other players in this TV race.

There is a hollow in the grill of programming of Spanish television. The managers are very worried and hopelessly looking for a new program. As young producer you must prepare your best TV program of varieties and you have a great list of personalities  for it. Contract the surrealistic Celebrities, discover to the public the most incredible histories and create a spectacular Plate to captivate the spectators. Only the program with more success will finish within the programming!

But care, the Celebrities that you can contract not only attracts the hearing. All has special qualities: Samantha Fox and Sabrina Salerno attract masculine  Celebrities of the program of an opponent, Bud Spencer can leave them out of action with a hand strike and you do not want to know what happens when Chuck Norris appears in action. Muchachada Nui is a card game for three or four players of thirteen years or more. You will be able to play a game of thirty to sixty minutes, but maybe you’ll get caught for some hours...

Hay un hueco en la parrilla de programación de televisión española. Los directivos están muy preocupados y buscan un nuevo programa desesperadamente. Como joven productor debes preparar tu mejor programa televisivo de variedades y para ello cuentas con un gran elenco de personalidades. Contrata a las más rutilantes Celebrities, descubre al público las historias más increíbles y crea un espectacular Plató para cautivar a la audiencia. ¡Sólo el programa con más éxito acabará dentro de la programación!

Muchachada Nui: El juego de mesa es un juego que se juega con cartas para tres o cuatro jugadores de trece años o más. Podréis echar una partida de treinta a sesenta minutos, aunque luego os liáis y os pueden dar las tantas.

description: "Panzer Ace Wittmann" is a two-player tactical wargame simulates the "Battle of Bocage" in June 13rd, 1944. Players lead German SS Heavy Panzer Battalion 101 and British 7th Armor Division respectively to fight for Villers Bocage. Each hex represents 50m across and each unit represents individual tank or artillery. This game use a "Time Point" driven mechanics and each time point represents one minute in real time. This game will be published on Board Wargame magazine issue #4 in early November, 2010.

description:
Billygoat Mountain is a light, fun "race to the top of the mountain game" for 2-6 players, and plays in around 15-20 mins.

The cards are randomly placed in a pyramid shape that forms a series of goat tracks leading to the top of a mountain.  However, not all paths head toward the top, some are rock-falls or dead-ends or lead in different directions.

Each player is a Billygoat, each of whom starts at the bottom of the mountain and must race their way up to the top.  The first Billygoat to reach the top is the winner.  However, as each player takes their turn, they change the paths leading to the top, to either help themselves or to hinder other players.

Extract: Rise of an Empire is a space themed empire building game with no random elements. Players compete for resources and advances to achieve victory. To build your empire you will need to build colonies and forges to extract and process numerous resources.

Extract: Rise of an Empire revolves around an ever changing economy that will make or ruin your best laid plans. Players can influence the economy directly as the resources available on the board directly dictate the state of the economy.

Will you match your opponents move for move or will you be three steps ahead scheming their financial woes? Will you buy into the market in hopes of increasing the value of your resources? Will your empire be the most advanced? Go forth and enjoy Extract: Rise of an Empire.

From the publisher

For less than the price of 1/2 hour of tutoring, your child can master all their basic math skills...and enjoy it way more.

Mathino is the ultimate math card game. Using math operations, capture the cards on the table with the cards from your hand. Players can change the values of the cards on the table on their turn, but watch out, someone else can change it to something that will let them capture that stack by the end of the round.

Mathino lets anyone sharpen their mental process by looking ahead a few moves to capture cards. It's a perfect math tutor and can be played by nearly anyone.

Operations used in the game can be determined based on player age and skill level and include:

-Matching shapes and colors, counting for the very young
-Addition
-Subtraction
-Multiplication
-Division
-Squaring and Cubing
-Square and Cube Rooting
-Sum of the Digits

LineUp! is played on a 5x5 grid of squares, with circles at each corner of these squares forming a 6x6 grid. On each player’s turn they are permitted to place a token on any of these squares or corners. Players start with 25 tokens each and the game is over when both players have used all of their tokens.

The object of the game is to attempt to build secured structures by either placing or capturing tokens on the board. There are two types of secured structures:

     Having tokens on a square and the 4 corners surrounding it
     Having tokens on a corner and the 4 squares surrounding it


Tokens making up a secured structure cannot be captured.

Instead of placing a token on an unoccupied area of the board, a player may remove one of their opponent’s tokens and replace it with their own. This is called capturing a token. Captured
tokens are removed from the game. A token may only be captured if you have a line of tokens adjacent to the token you wish to capture that is greater in length than the line of your
opponent’s adjacent tokens in the opposite direction. Thus, to capture a square you must have a line of squares and to capture a corner you must have a line of corners. Tokens that make up
part of a secured structure cannot be captured, but can be used in forming lines to capture other tokens.

The game ends when both players have run out of tokens to place on the board. When this happens, count the number of secured structures each player has built during the game. Remember, tokens can be used in more than one secured structure. The player with the highest number of secured structures wins.

If the number of secured structures is equal, then the player with the largest number of tokens on the board wins on possession (this is the same as the player who has captured the most tokens). If this number is equal then the game is a draw.

(Excerpts from the instructions)

TAMGA(Mark, Clan-Banner) board game description:
The players take the role of clan leaders of Anatolia and try to gain control of enemy clan leaders' capital cities to become the ultimate ruler of an empire. If a player loses his capital city, he is left out of the game. There are optional rules for winning: world domination, timed game play etc. The game map resembles RISK but the Tamga game map covers territories from eastern Europe, Anatolia and northern Africa. Battle mechanics involve dice rolling, army hiring and paying their upkeep cost, buying culture cards to be able to hire more powerful armies. Each clan has an army-location-figure to represent their army on the game map. A player can not have more than one army. The army of a player consists of maximum 3 squads. (Janissaries, Cavalry, Ranged attackers etc.)  Specific cities have to be captured to hire specific squads. More powerful squads can be hired from more powerful neutral cities. A player has to defeat the militia of a neutral city to be able to hire those squads. The Tamga game comes with 6-sided(d6) special dice. Each die have three Star faces, two Crescent faces and one Sword face. Different squads have to roll different symbols to be successful. The cards representing the squads have special abilities against other squads written on them. More powerful armies require cultural achievements. The culture cards have historical flavor text on them and a culture point value at the bottom. A player has to pay money(AKCE) to buy culture cards and hire squads. The game comes with a small pouch filled with yellow and white metal coins called Akce. The coins are designed true to the original coins of era.

(from the official Tamga board game website):
TAMGA is a tactical strategic board game about the Ottoman Empire that takes place during from its the foundation until the end of its golden era. Players take role of Turkish leaders and try to defeat other players to become the ultimate ruler of Ottoman Empire.

The flavor texts on game cards, the money currency of the game called "akce" pieces, drawings of soldiers and Ottoman Empire game map are the result of hundreds of hours of research from historical literature.

The creation of game's historical texts and design took nearly four years of careful research and reading. During this time Associate Professor Erhan Afyoncu have been consulted numerous times to provide a true to the original historical experience for the players.

Munchkin Sammlerkoffer is a metal briefcase designed to hold all of your Munchkin cards (until they make more), all of the Munchkin dice (until they make more), all of the bookmarks and rule books (until...), and all of the other extraneous Munchkin coins, pens and doodads tucked around your game room.

Munchkin Sammlerkoffer, which succeeds the out-of-print and previously large enough Munchkin Sammlerbox, includes a Munchkin Kill-O-Meter for you, its owner, to keep track of your fighting level as well as 101 rare or brand new German-language Munchkin cards: 55 (of the 56) cards in Munchkin 7: More Good Cards, a selection of German promotional cards not previously available outside of fairs and conventions, the promotional cards from the German Munchkin Bites!-Halloween cooperation with 20th Century Fox Home Entertainment, all cards designed for the Munchkin Sammlerbox, and 13 new cards unique to the Sammerkoffer and illustrated by John Kovalic.

As Munchkin Sammlerkoffer is a magic item – yes, really – it grants its owner a special ability in each Munchkin game.

Reimplements:

    Munchkin Sammlerbox


				
				
					Unofficial Sammlerkoffer FAQ:(Courtesy of Clipper)

This should encompass all the information a Munchkin fan wants to know about the box, its included cards and accessories and so on.

What is the Sammlerkoffer?
The Sammlerkoffer (translation: Collector's Case) is a product released by the German licensee of the Munchkin property, Pegasus Spiele. It is a metal suitcase-style box with a plastic insert that is perfect for holding the cards included in the Munchkin card games. It also comes with some German cards and a Kill-O-Meter.

Where do I get the Sammlerkoffer?
Pegasus Spiele's shop is the easiest way to order it. See the "Links" section for the link to the game page, which has a link to the shop page. The site is mostly in German, but there is a translation option at the top right that makes it easy enough to get through the ordering process without knowing any German (note that it will not translate the product description). Note that you will not pay during the checkout process. Instead, Pegasus Spiele will contact you (using English) and give you instructions on how to pay via either bank transfer or Paypal.

How much does it cost?
It costs 39,95 Euro plus an additional 15 Euro shipping to most locations (at least to the US and Australia, Europe might be less). Payment must be made by either International Bank Transfer or Paypal as specified in the previous question.

What else do you get with it?
There are 101 included cards with German text and backs for various sets. There is also a Kill-O-Meter. The Sammlerkoffer also has its own special rule.

What is the Sammlerkoffer's special rule?
If you are playing with cards that all come from a single Sammlerkoffer, the owner of the Sammlerkoffer may, once per game, take a card from either draw pile at any time that they are not in combat. If more than one person brings a Sammlerkoffer, then play with cards from the heavier Sammlerkoffer.

Are the cards usable with English Munchkin sets?
Some of them can be mixed into your existing sets, but the German backs for some cards are different from the original versions, so they would stand out.

     The backs of the Munchkin cards, both Treasures and Doors, are significantly darker than the English versions.
     The card backs for the following games have the game name in German: Munchkin Bites!; The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin; and Munchkin Booty.
     The cards with the same backs are for Star Munchkin; Super Munchkin; Munchkin Cthulhu; and Munchkin Fu.


How many cards are there for each themed set?
The breakdown is as follows:



    
         Munchkin: 45 Doors and 30 Treasures

    
    
         Super Munchkin: 1 Door and 1 Treasure

    
    
         Munchkin Bites!: 6 Doors and 1 Treasure

    
    
         Munchkin Cthulhu: 2 Doors and 4 Treasures

    
    
         The Good, the Bad, and the Munchkin: 1 Treasure

    
    
         Star Munchkin: 3 Doors and 1 Treasure

    
    
         Munchkin Booty: 2 Doors and 1 Treasure

    
    
         Munchkin Fu: 3 Doors

    


Can sleeved cards fit into the Sammlerkoffer?
Some can, but it is a tight fit. The 56mm x 87mm standard sleeves from Mayday just fit inside the box. Nobody has reported yet whether the premium version of the sleeves or if other types of sleeves fit or not. Note that the sleeved cards do take up more room, so you will get about 25% less cards in the box.

How many cards fit inside the Sammlerkoffer?
By my estimate, approximately 4,000 unsleeved cards or 3,000 sleeved cards can fit if you pack them tightly. This is not counting the areas set aside for rules, dice and Dungeon cards.

How are the card areas arranged?
The plastic base of the Sammlerkoffer includes spaces for various decks and expansions. The included insert suggests putting each core set and expansion in a different slot, but as the ratio of decks to expansions is approximately equivalent to the ratio between Doors and Treasures, it is possible to use a pair of slots (one large, one medium) for the Doors and Treasures of sets with one 112 card expansion or two 56 card expansions, which is perfect for most of the Munchkin themes. The number of slots and approximate number of unsleeved cards that each slot can hold are as follows:

     11 slots for core sets or Doors of an expanded set fitting about 190 cards each.
     12 slots for 112 card expansions or Treasures of an expanded set fitting about 125 cards each.
     6 slots for Boosters and Promo cards fitting about 60 cards each.
     4 slots for Dungeon cards fitting about 40 Dungeon cards each.
     1 slot for storing rules, dice, bookmarks, Kill-O-Meters, pawns and whatever else you want to store.


Is the Kill-O-Meter the same as the Steve Jackson Games version?
Not quite. It features the same art, but it uses different card stock.

What icons are on the cards?
All 101 cards have an icon for the Sammlerkoffer. In addition, 55 of the cards have the More Good Cards icon, one card has the Kill-O-Meter icon, and one card has the Crazed Caverns icon.

What are the translations of the included cards?
We do not provide a translation of the cards here. That being said, all but 18 of the cards have already been translated or have equivalent cards in English (see the "Community Wiki" section, below, for hints on where to look).

This is the WW2 edition of the Ambush miniature rules series.

It is a complete game, including fully integrated infantry and armour rules that can be played in a reasonable time. Use of an hex grid recommended for smoother play.
Smaller tables can be played in as little as 45 minutes.
Infantry three stand units represent platoons or full companies and they do play their proper role; like in reality mighty tanks are of little use unless properly supported. This is effectively enforced by awarding full objective values only when infantry takes them.
The game does not feature common alternate turns, instead HQ issue orders accordingly to random initiative dice (d6); this means that HQ Ally1 may activate, then HQ Axis1, then HQ Ally2, etc.   
The rules feature 1:144 miniature pictures and are probably best from 1:200 to 1:144.

Because of its design and the use of an hex grid by default, this game has been pointed as a bridge between a miniture wargame and a boardgame.

Note: this is a rules book only, an hex grid, a dozen d6, 1 d20 dice and miniatures or custom built counters required to play.

Now available again (2023) from Wargame Vault, in pdf format.

Has been available on BGG market, in book format.

Het spel van de Eeuw (The Game of the Century) is a trivia game with questions regarding persons, events and developments from the 20th century.

The questions are arranged in five categories. The game is played on a board depicting 60 fields, arranged in a circle. Each field has a colour corresponding to a category; every sixth field is a large scoring field.

On a player's turn, he rolls the die and moves his pawn as many spaces in the direction he chooses. He is then asked a question from the category of the field he landed on. If he answers the question correctly, he gets to move again.

If a player lands on a scoring field and manages to answer the question correctly, he not only gets to move again, but he also receives a century chip of the correct colour (if he doesn't have one in that colour yet). The first player to collect century chips of all colours wins the game.

The game can be expanded by adding questions from the various editions of Spel van het Jaar (Game of the Year).

The name of the game is DownsideUp. It is played and scored like rummy, but with a vengeful twist.  Players are dealt a hand of cards, with the goal being to rid yourself of all the cards in your hand. You can do this by playing cards to the table, in groups of three, either in sets (groups of cards with the game value from different suits) or in runs (three or more consecutive cards for one suit). Once a person lays down a group of cards, other players -- including the player who played the original set -- can now "lay off" cards to that group.

The unique components of this game are:

1.    Negative Cards - When you think your opponent has amassed too many points and is about to win the game, take revenge and play your negative cards to take points away for your opponent.

2.    Discarding your card Downsideup - If you have been watching which cards your opponent is collecting and you think your discard is going to help them, then play your discard “Downsideup” to keep your opponent from seeing that card, but remember where you put it so you can play it again later.

3.    Wild Cards - When your opponent lays down the card you have been waiting for to complete their run or set of card, use a wild card instead to play those cards to score points.

Almost all of the events portrayed in this game actually took place in Washington DC during the 1980s. The exact circumstances and names have been changed to protect the guilty. This game portrays the fauna of the District of Chaos and the institutions of its home rule government in realistic situations. Some parts of this game may be unsuitable for the politically naive. Home Rulette is a political parody game themed on political corruption. In the game, players move their pieces along the path based on dice rolls. Other players can impede movement through card-play. Cards are gained by landing on spaces. The first player to reach the end space is the winner. “Home Rulette takes place in the District of Chaos, a make believe capitol city of a world super power. The game is totally fictional. Obviously nothing so ridiculous as the characters, events or institutions contained in this game could exist in the real world.This make-believe capital city had been created by the country’s Founding Fathers as a neutral place, free from local political funny-business, where people could safely go and conduct the nation’s business.

However, this concept had one fatal flaw: it worked, and as we all know, its standard government policy to try and fix things that aren’t broken. As with any congressional action, many interest groups had to be satisfied. One of these groups was the Humor Lobby. If improvements had to be made, they should be funny. Congress thought long and hard on what could be done to generate the most amount of laughter over the longest period of time. Then the idea came to them. Why not take the capital city which belongs to all of the country’s citizens, and hand it over to a few local people who tried to burn it down in the 1960’s”. At this suggestion smiles broke out on the Congressmen’s faces as the potential for a massive comic effect rolled through their minds. Then like drunk Olympian gods who decided to reach down from the heavens and play some tricks on some unfortunate mortals, they passed a…..bill. The results were as anticipated. The ‘wheeling, dealing and stealing’ which occurred as a result of this act kept the city and the country laughing for years. The District of Chaos lurched from misadventure, to atrocity, to debacle, and then back again, as the inmates played government. To quote his excellency Mayor-For-Life Marion Larry at his third coronation, “The government can give people the change, …but it’s up to the individuals to take advantage.” This game gives you, the ordinary citizen, the chance to “take advantage.” What happens when a coalition of welfare entrepreneurs, black supremacists, radical homosexuals & common criminals take over the local government of the national capital city of a modern superpower? Wheel, Deal & Steal Bash Chinks Blackmail Opponents Spread a fatal social disease Flim-flam the Feds Harass Honkeys Come Alive, Dis Ain’t no jive Screw a Honky, Vote De Donkey

Become an orchard farmer and earn a living selling your crop at market.  Rise early each morning to pick fruit from your trees and load up your market cart.  Spend too much time picking produce and you will be the last farmer to arrive at the market.

Apples, oranges, pears, plums, and apricots are all nearly in season.  Market patrons line up, rain or shine, to get the best fruit at its ripest.

Make a profit by attracting the right customers.  Meet the demands of your patrons by trading fruit with your fellow farmers.  Help another farmer out when you can because cashing in on a favour is crucial to bringing your victory to fruition.

The "knickers in a twist" game, where knickers is the English term for ladies underwear.

Adult variant of Twister. Players have to place their hands and feet on colored spaces on the game mat as indicated by a spinner, but the forfeit for failing to place a hand or foot in a coloured area is to remove (or put back on) an item of clothing.

Source of description: publishers rulebook.

Re-implements:

    Twister

Choose your EVOKER and evoke powerful ENTI to defeat your rival. ENTI (plural for ENTIS) are powerful beings from another realm, the Dream Realm, who aid you in battle. You can also call upon hordes of monsters in the meantime to protect and strike for you!

Basic Play Rules

-Each player must have a deck of at least 30 cards. At least 1 of those cards MUST be an EVOKER card. The minimum is 30 but you may have more, as many as you wish.

-There are no doubles allowed of EVOKER or ENTIS cards (you may have 2 cards of the
same name as long as the illustration is different) but you may have up to 3 copies of all MONSTER/BOSS and SKILL cards.

-Each player starts with 90HP.

Select an EVOKER to start then shuffle your deck and draw 5 cards (this applies to both starting players).

Roll a dice to see who plays their EVOKER first. If its you draw a card and play an ENTIS or MONSTER/BOSS card(s). You may not attack on your 1st turn (this applies to both starting players).
When both players have set-up defenses, ENTIS or MONSTER/BOSS card(s) then another dice roll is done and whoever has the highest dice roll may attack and have their 2nd turn.

After that, turns just alternate accordingly.

How a Battle Turn Goes...
1.Draw a card.
2.Play an EVOKER, ENTIS and/or MONSTER/BOSS card(s).
3.Play any Add-Ons or SKILL cards.
4.Attack (Enter Battle Mode)
5.Play ONLY any other SKILL cards you wish.
6.Turn end.

An Encounter (the entire course of your game) proceeds until one of you loses all their HP or a player can no longer draw cards.

02/02/11

        NEW**
    


Theme Decks out!
Starlit Night Deck: Use a deck brimming with the chaotic energy of the stars!

Harness the unpredictable power of the stars (Star-Type Cards) in this all new strategically structured theme deck. Use "Affuse" to evoke and then re-evoke fallen Enti all the while playing lady-luck by using Star Monsters and their random, high-risk effect Abilities.

If you like to tempt fate (while using "Marlain" to redo your die rolls) then this deck is for you. Watch as your opponent's well laid plans crumble.

OR

Darkside Moon Deck: Decimate foes with raw power and the inverse strategy of dark matter!

Darkness and destruction await all those who face the power of the moon (Moon-Type Cards). This deck while lacking in Enti power relies on hordes of Monster/Boss Cards particularly "Cronie" a dark bird who returns automatically once defeated.

Master dark matter and increase your attack power, all the while using inverse tactics to raise your HP and reduce your opponents. Without having to lift a single finger...

Players assume the roles of Shrek, Princess Fiona, Donkey, or Puss in Boots and travel through Shrek's fairytale world.

Each player tries to collect the 4 items (unique combinations of character, place and thing cards) needed to complete their Fairytale card.

Movement through the fairytale world is by rolling a die. A player landing at a crossroad spins the spinner (3 are embedded in the board) to find out which way he must travel. After the player collects his 4 items, he must be the first to return to Shrek's ship to win the game.

Once on the ship, the Fairytale card is turned over and the previously unseen story is read aloud, substituting the items on the collected cards as indicated in the story. Example: "There once was a sweet little lass named {Character 1} who lived in {Place 1}..." might become "There once was a sweet little lass named Pinocchio who lived in Smelly Swamp..."
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Puss in Boots

description:
"Match pairs of chihuahuas in this fast-paced fiesta of fun. Send single pups to the pound but watch out! If someone calls Andale! Andale! all of the pint-sized pooches are up for grabs. Be quick to grab the matching mutts before other players can get their paws on them. The player with the most matches wins!"

Source : back of package

Andale! Andale! is a rummy-based children's game, players put down matches as they draw them (first player to five matches wins). To mix it up there are a few special cards (trade, steal, and Andale!). When someone plays an Andale! card, players rush to grab the pile of discarded cards that they want.

boardgame: War Rocket: Space Battles in the Atomic Age
description:
From the Hydra web site:

War Rocket is a fast-paced miniature space combat game set in the retro sci-fi future of the 24th century. The brave Galacteers fight to protect mankind from sinister alien forces. Will you command a fleet of Galecteer rockets or choose the forces of the domineering Valkeeri, the brutal Imperium of Marduk, or the enigmatic Zenithians?

You can play a game of War Rocket with fleets of 10-20 ships in less than 90 minutes with minimal use of tokens and absolutely no paper record keeping!

The 78-page War Rocket rulebook also includes 8 scenarios with specific objectives to encourage tactical decision making and narrative campaigns.

The rules also include advanced rules include special actions such as boarding and ramming, space stations, and space objects such as planetoids and asteroid fields.

Rocket customization rules allow you to upgrade your rockets with options such as force fields, lucky pilots, and disruptor beams. You can also use these rules to create your own new fleets.

The game board shows shelves with pairs of item. One of each pair is eco-friendly (and has a green dot), the other isn't (and has a red dot). The items are also depicted on tiles, and there are 15 additional garbage tiles.

The item tiles are placed face-down around the game board. The active player flips open a tile. If the depicted item is covered by a garbage tile on the board, the turn ends - but if it is a tile with a red dot, another item in the shelves has to be covered. If the item is still free, another tile can be flipped open. If the two tiles form a pair, the green-dotted card is put on a shelf , the red one is discarded. Also, a garbage tile can be removed from the board. If the two tiles don't match, the player has to cover all red-dotted items s/he just flipped open with garbage tiles.

When all items with green dots are covered with item tiles, the game ends. If there are still garbage cards on the shelves, all players lose. If not, everyone wins.

(User summary by HilkMAN)

Thruway is a two-player connection game with many twists and turns. Two not-so-civil engineers attempt to build a thruway across the landscape while his or her opponent contests the right of way.

The game of Thruway requires the following components:


        120 road section tiles; 60 gray concrete tiles and 60 black asphalt tiles.
        One 11 x 11 square landscape game board with 121 parcels of land cells.


The landscape game board is empty at the beginning of the game.

The concrete player tries to connect the east and west borders. The asphalt player tries to connect the north and south borders.

The concrete player plays first. Then on the asphalt player's first turn only, if he or she feels the concrete player has too much advantage from the placement of the first two concrete tiles, the asphalt player may opt to trade tiles and roles. The asphalt player becomes the concrete player and vice versa.  Whether or not roles are reversed, the asphalt player then takes a turn. Player turns then alternate for the rest of the game. Players may not pass a turn unless they have no legal move. Consecutive passes can never occur.

During a turn, an engineer may do any one of the following three actions:
1.    Pave a road without replacement,
2.    Pave a road with replacement, or 
3.    Resolve a crossroads dispute by swapping tiles.

To pave a road without replacement, a player places two of his or her road sections on two horizontally or vertically adjacent empty parcels of land.

To pave a road with replacement, a player places two of his or her road sections on two horizontally or vertically adjacent parcels of land, where one parcel is empty and the other cell contains an opponent's road section.  The replaced road section is recycled to its owner's reserve.

When a 2 x 2 square of land parcels is occupied by four road sections where asphalt and concrete roads occupy the two diagonals, this is called a crossroads dispute.

Crossroads Disputes
ox    
xo or

xo
ox

A player may resolve a crossroads dispute in either of two ways:
1.    by swapping any asphalt and concrete road sections along an edge of the 2 x 2 square, or,
2.    if possible, by paving with replacement.

When swapping is used to resolve the dispute, only one swap per player turn is allowed. New crossroad disputes may arise due to the resolution of a dispute. In this case, the resolution of the newly formed dispute(s) may not immediately recreate the first dispute. This restriction, which prevents position repetition, applies only to your opponent's immediate response. On later player turns, the resolution can be reversed by either player.
      
A road section is indestructible, only if, all its adjacent north, south, east and west land parcels (that exist on the game board) have been paved by either player and it is not part of a crossroads dispute.

Goal: The first civil engineer to pave a thruway (connected path of horizontally and/or vertically adjacent indestructible road sections) connecting their two sides of the landscape wins the game.

Hexabrae is an abstract strategic game in which players try to enclose a part of the gameboard with their playing pieces. The first player that is able to make an enclosure using more of his/her own playing pieces than opponent pieces, wins the game.

Playing pieces can have 1 of 3 possibile orientations. The orientation of a piece dictates in which directions it can move. Besides moving it is also possible to change the orientation of pieces during play.

This is an open ended action game.

The object of the game is to be the first player to raise the "Ideal" flag.  Players do this by arranging the various mechanisms, drums, and obstacles and then try to run a ball bearing through their course.

Steel balls go down the ramp or "Bingleflinger," bounce on one or more of the "Hum Drums," go through the "Flickerticker," bounce on more "Hum Drums" through/around the "Banglevator" and eventually into the "Boinglebucket" to raise the IDEAL flag. If unsuccessful, then the next player gets to move a piece and make an attempt at raising the flag.  Play continues likewise until someone devises a successful system of components and raises the flag.  The directions actually have several games you can play against other players and score.

boardgame: The World Cup Game: Expansion Set 6
description:
This expansion for The World Cup Game includes South Africa 2010 World Cup boards and the new 20 card extension pack.

Release date is mid-October 2010.

(translates from Greek as the army disbander)

In 411b.c. Aristophanes wrote his theatrical comedy Lysistrata. It is one of the oldest anti-war plays. The show is about the sexual strike which is declared by the women in order to convince their men to stop the peloponnesian war.This game is based on the theatrical plot and on how each side is trying to assert itself against the other.

Contents 86 cards, 1 board, 6 pawns, 1 rulebook.

Players alternate turns while trying to surpass their opponents
in four forms of conflict: voting, influence, charisma and memory.
Each conflict is dealt with, like in theatrical plays. The leader of the chorus has the initiative, or everyone starts appearing in order on the scene, or the appearance is apparently random. 
There are two ways to win: By surpassing certain forms of conflict or by being the first to win 7 conflicts of any kind.

A game for the people and their right to protest! Here you have it! The board game of rally, riot, protest and riot control, not necessarily in that order. What would you do? If all the people in a city that have something to protest about, actually did? Seven different rally groups are marching the streets and numerous heat squads are trying to maintain order while tension rises to a peak. Reporters are exposing the facts in the media sometimes in a good way, other times in a way that seems like slander to some. In Urbania, you are in control of a group of people that march to protest about their rights. During that march you will clash with heat squads, reporters, other rally groups and the ultimate choice: to lead your group to vindication, or accept the rule of the system and maintain order in your city. Order and chaos in a game of urban instability.

Contains: 24 city squares 7 player’s groups 110 cards 2 six sided dice 35 tokens 63 markers 1 victory track 1 rulebook

AWARDS.
Urbania has been awarded with the best theme and best game mechanic award in BGG's 1st Greek board game design contest.

prelude

During the middle ages, a powerful wizard obtained a unique item after years of searching. He secretly hid it in a royal treasury hall, that no one dared to enter.That was one of the most important tasks of his life. Centuries later, an archaeologist excavated the same item the wizard had placed in the treasury hall. The archaeologist, also hid the item in a secret place called the Continuum Vault. His purpose was the same as the wizard’s. To leave the item in the Vault, until the proper passing of the centuries, like a good wine in a cellar. In the far future, an agent that had access to the continuum vault, extracted the item she had asked from the wizard and the  archaeologist to keep safe through the centuries.

THE GUILD. The Wizard was a founding member of a Wizard’s Guild that managed the impossible. To travel without moving. Although, the moving wasn’t through space. The Guild discovered that when you manage to travel without using the dimensions of space, you travel through time. Hence, the new name of the Guild was based on the event of that movement through time, the Total Inertia Moving Event Guild, or simply T I M E Guild. Centuries passed, the coming of the Nano Age totally reformed the Guild into a T I M E Agency and gave a new agenda to its members: The molecular processing. The Agency could combine almost anything in order to create new properties it could utilize. But, some items needed to be centuries old in order to evolve or to become suitable for the processing. Therefore, the Agency decided to cooperate with its members from times past in order to bring items to the future in their desirable state. So, there were many ways for one to do that. You could simply travel back in time instruct someone in the past, to hide an item for you in a place that you knew, and travel back in your time and take the item, which now would be centuries old. Or, you could take an item from your time and hide it somewhere in the past by yourself and take it back when you return in the future. But, the T I M E Agency has many members, so that it can function properly in all Eras. If you travel back in time, too far away from your Era, it could be hard to adjust in the ways people had in the past, so, the help of another person from that Era makes the procedure easier. Usually, a Mission that includes the keeping of an item through the centuries, is concluded by 3 members: A Wizard, an Archaeologist, and a T I M E Agent specialized in traveling through the ages.

Components.
1 board.
4 player sheets.
12 pawns (3 for each player).
108 cards.
1 rulebook.

GAME-PLAY. 
All players begin the game with a mission that requires at least 4 items of different properties. Each player during his/er round uses his/er 3 pawns, which are the Wizard, the Archaeologist and the T I M E traveler. The board of the game is divided in three regions that represent three aspects of time: Past Present and Future. Each pawn starts from one of these regions and moves in order. First the Wizard can move in regions of the Past to collect information about powerful items and artifacts and then gather the items and/or artifacts for which he possesses the proper information. Then, the Archaeologist, moves in the Present regions in which he can also gather items that are somewhat different from the Past items and he can gather some more info about artifacts of the Past that might help the Wizard find them. Last, the Time Traveler moves and starts from the future areas, in which she can also gather info and items. The difference in the future region is that, items gathered here are built for time travel, which means that they evolve as the centuries pass. It is best for the Time traveler to travel back in time and leave these items in the Past or the Present to age and become stronger. Each item in Continuum Run has tiers, 3 sometimes 4. When an item is new, it begins from tier 3. Some areas on the board allow the players to store items and take them back from another time period. For example in the Present regions there is the Continuum Vault. If the Archaeologist stores an item of tier 3 in the Present it stays there until someone takes it. The Continuum Vault has its future version, in which the time traveler can go and take the item the Archaeologist has stored. But, centuries have passed, so the item must be replaced with a lower tier. Tier 2 in this case. Different tiers have different values. So, if a player requires for his/er mission an item with a value of Ruination 3, and s/he possesses a future item that has Ruination 2, s/he may store that item in one region and take it back from the next.
GOAL.
Any player that has at least 4 items that his/er mission requires may cause an endgame, during which, a last round is played and then players score Victory Points for each item and/or artifact they have managed to send to the Future Area called Molecular Processor.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings Strategy Battle Game: Gondor in Flames
